assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item related to testing
assertion_results.evidence: The four short-term items (A1–A4) cover Sentry, uptime monitoring, branch protection, and code review policy. No testing action item appears in the short-term horizon. The first testing item (A6: 'Write integration tests for top 3 incident-prone API endpoints') is explicitly marked medium-term.

assertion_results.text: Output must mention the absence of a staging environment
assertion_results.evidence: Executive summary bullet: 'No staging environment means every deploy is a live experiment on real users.' Also listed as blocker #3: 'No staging environment — untested code ships directly to production users, amplifying every other risk.'

assertion_results.text: Output must score CI/CD at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: Area scores table: 'CI/CD | 2/5 | GitHub Actions exists but enforces no quality gate'. Score is exactly 2/5.

assertion_results.text: Output must recommend a specific test type to adopt first, not just 'add tests'
assertion_results.evidence: Medium-term section: 'start with integration tests for the Node.js API using Jest + Supertest, targeting the three most incident-prone endpoints first.' The specific test type (integration tests), framework (Jest + Supertest), scope (API), and targeting criteria are all named.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend a full test suite rewrite as the first step
assertion_results.evidence: The first testing recommendation is targeted and incremental: integration tests on the three most incident-prone endpoints, with explicit guidance to 'Don't aim for coverage percentage — aim for the paths that have burned the team.' No full rewrite is suggested at any stage.
